item.title: Who runs the world?
item.description: From Toni Nady https://www.instagram.com/p/CanduCBF5rF/ See also: Dance tutorial of the “BlackRock Top Rock” https://imgur.com/a/GKoDrXL I think I'm gonna need a lot more of the disturbing facts about the world explained to me by cute, weird ladies doing interpretive dance, from now on. Maybe I should try interpretive dance explanations instead of trying to hit people with some meme-based knowledge: https://imgur.com/gallery/7S8CnMo If you want to fight this, it's pretty simple. You've already completed the hardest step, which is realising and accepting what's going on. -Join your union. -Switch to industry-owned superannuation. -Join your Labor party and help out where you can. -Connect with other like-minded people and organise for change.

item.dc:creator: Yoda1PositivityStar

item.title: 7/7/22 freedom
item.description: Thursday I had to make the decision to leave. My husband of 13 years was not a man I recognized, he was paranoid, controlling and began destroying property and spending all the money. I brought the cops out for an involuntary mental admittance and they found meth on him. That was it. That was the cause of my year from hell. Restraining order is up, he is gone. Now my job is to get out of the financial ruin he left me in. Fixing the property he destroyed, and repairing the relationships with people I was not allowed to talk to. Mainly loving myself again and that I was not the problem or cause. It has been freeing to be able to openly talk to people without fear that my phone would be smashed or my cars interior would be slashed up more.
item.dc:creator: mentallydrowning

item.title: Mormon student pranks his Pro-Life mom into thinking he got a stripper pregnant and mom tells him to have her get an abortion
item.description:
A BYU student was paid $500 to prank call his Mormon pro-life mother and try to convince her he got a stripper pregnant. It didn't take long to convince her of that part—she didn't seem to have any issue believing her conservative Mormon son would have sex with strippers. Instead, she immediately began questioning the imaginary woman in the situation, asking how she could possibly know she was pregnant already which means this woman, this mother, understands it often takes weeks before you know you're pregnant. A caption in the TikTok video points out how many states have rules in place that expect a woman to know at or before 4 weeks. After dwelling on the time-line for a moment, the mother immediately advises her son to tell the woman to get an abortion.
The young man, shocked at how quickly and nonchalantly his mother tossed abortion out as the solution—regardless of legality, the strong moral and religious stance she's allegedly held or accessibility issues since this is Utah and clearly not a case of rape or incest—questioned if she was serious. "Mom, no!" he gasped. Without missing a beat, pro-lifer mom confirmed she was serious. Then she asked where he found a stripper, throwing a little extra disgust on it as if the imaginary woman carrying her imaginary grandchild was something to be spat on. He tried making up a story, but eventually his shock got the better of him and he told her the truth about the prank. Mom hung up wordlessly. The clip was reposted to TikTok as a reminder "that rich White conservatives will ALWAYS have access to safe abortions, and have no problem utilizing this privilege when it impacts them." Source with video: https://www.comicsands.com/mormon-prolife-mom-stripper-pregnant-2657654938.html
item.dc:creator: Igotfourbedrats

item.title: Accomplices to Murder
item.description:
Source: https://twitter.com/Imposter_Edits/status/1547364174124224521
She brought the fire
I hope it got through to them
Couldn't find any info on who this is. Can't reform the police if the people in charge don't take action. The InContext YouTube page is here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=toTZ2VmV4sY Happy edit: @mayorofdrixdale found out who she is: "Her name is Naira Tamminga. https://www.woodtv.com/news/grand-rapids/i-am-frustrated-gr-teens-powerful-speech-at-city-meeting-goes-viral/" Wanna give their comment an upvote? item.title: Prime Bezos' Wallet Day is more like it
item.description: Some small businesses list on Amazon just to be found. Ships/Sold By Amazon: Amazon purchased this wholesale and is reselling. Ships from Amazon, sold by XYZ: XYZ is paying Amazon to stock and deliver their products. Ships/Sold By XYZ: Third party seller listing on Amazon. They pay a fee to Amazon on sales. These are the small businesses that need your support.
item.dc:creator: SpecialBrownies

item.title: Just more enraging pictures from the Uvalde surveillance video.
item.description:
Punisher Lock Screen douchebag.
Well there it is, the most disturbing sentence I’ve read all year.
Hand sanitizer: For when you need to clean the blood of children off your hands.
Not one, but two motherfuckers on their phones.
WHAT POSSIBLE FUCKING REASON IS HE LAUGHING FOR?
It’s been two months now and every new piece of info keeps burying these Uvalde cops into the hole that they dug for themselves. https://twitter.com/jenny2x4/status/1547087502518865921?s=21&t=31lJ6yyXLZyjN4OcUzqhEQ

item.description: I was in an abusive relationship. I will never regret my decisions, it was the right choice. Protect #abortion
item.dc:creator: JoeyKillface

item.title: Coming out as trans at work and publicly tomorrow. Wish me luck?
item.description: Figured I was actually a lady (albeit a foul-mouthed one) three years ago. Tomorrow I'm finally sharing this fact with everyone at work, as well as the public, after having been closely closeted for a while. Excuse the crap stress doodle art. Wish me luck? I rarely post anything, but figured this was the sort of thing a good millenial should post about the night before the fact. In 9h, if all goes well, my new name will be updated to the company's systems, and I'll send an email explaining why my name's suddenly different in our chat client and so forth. Then cometh ye olde coming out post on social media. I'm pretty excited, but nervous. Hiding who you are can feel like the safest thing to do, but most of the time it takes more energy than being yourself. Anyways, be good to eachother!
item.dc:creator: BitchCake

item.title: Youth Coach Fired After Intentionally Hurting 9-Year Olds On Team He Lost To - He's A Cop.
item.description:
No words.
No, I literally have no words. Fuck this guy. And he's a fucking cop, no less. I mean, of course he is. Jesus. All because he couldn't stand losing to a different team of 9 year olds. Naturally he's been fired from being a coach to a Youth league, but abusing 9 year olds on video out of spite will no doubt never be enough to get him even suspended from his job. Can almost see all his buddies high-fiving him at the station as I type this. Kenneth Wendt. EDIT - UPDATE: "Prospect Baseball coach Victor Torres told the Chronicle that his team and his player's parents were not aware of the incident until they saw the Scorpions' second game on Saturday, which Wendt was allegedly thrown out of. "After they played us, they were scheduled to play another game," Torres said. "When they were playing that game, [Wendt] was harassing one of the kids that was playing first base. He even told him that he was going to meet him in the parking lot." Torres said that after one of the opposing players complained about Wendt in the dugout, the parent that took the original video of Wendt's aggressive behavior started to raise concerns. Torres then discovered Wendt was law enforcement. Upon getting thrown out of the game, Wendt repeatedly said he was "a cop," according to Torres." Source(s): https://www.khou.com/article/news/local/little-league-baseball-coach-fired-rough-encounter-opposing-players-after-loss/285-d9898a7e-7b3d-46bc-a058-6c38bf4999a6 https://www.houstonchronicle.com/neighborhood/spring-klein/article/little-league-coach-viral-video-17299334.php

item.title: A happy dog story and meme dump to fix your day
item.description:
A good boy through and through
he still won
he still takes hers I'm pretty sure
support
best hat
Hey guys and gals, we're still trying to support the friend that saved me from suicide earlier this year in the effort of getting his little pup healthy and happy There's an update in the campaign about the progress and it's promissing so we may not be on it much longer. If you can help them out, please do! And have a great day https://gofund.me/96113348
item.dc:creator: thedeadredhead

item.title: America’s Accidental Al-Qaeda (the case for designating elements of the US pro-Life movement as an extremist terrorist organisation)
item.description:
Most Viral Edit: Thank you UserSub. One of the most common insults being thrown around today, is that members of the far right Christians groups in America remind folks of the Taliban; the original comparison emerged in an episode of the early 2000’s TV show, ‘The Newsroom’, penned by Aaron Sorkin, and the comparison has stuck around since (to be precise the original insult was directed at ‘The Tea Party’ faction of the Republicans). However, the Taliban are only an ethno-nationalist group; their campaigns are focused on Afghanistan alone. There does exists, however, extremist Christian groups in America, who operate globally, spending hundreds of millions of dollars to manipulate people and who, above all, ARE responsible for the deaths of a LOT of innocent women around the world. That’s a heck of an accusation. This post intends to do prove it. To provide evidence to illustrate that these extremist Christian groups in the United States are not America’s version of the Taliban at all. They are America’s version of al-Qaeda- religious fanatics who operate globally and are very deadly. There are a LOT of links backing up every single thing said at the end of this down below. So we start the case against them by asking what appears to be an odd question, but which becomes really important as we go along- what is the number one cause of death of teenage girls on Earth?
According to the World Health Organisation, “Complications during pregnancy and childbirth are the leading cause of death for 15–19 year old girls globally”. Read that sentence back again dudes- THE leading cause of death for 15–19 year-old girls GLOBALLY. Bar nothing. Studies by UNESCO, and the WHO, estimate that every year 21 million girls aged between 15 to 19, specifically in the developing regions of the world, become pregnant; approximately 12 million of them give birth. At least 777,000 births occur to adolescent girls younger than 15 years old. This is horrendous. Some good news? The estimated global adolescent-specific fertility rate has declined by 11.6% over the past 20 years. Which IS good news, but it shows that awareness of this issue is a recent thing. We have made strides, and we will continue to make strides, but dear Lord, we have a long way to go. Now, I KNOW most folks on Imgur do not need to be told the reasons why adolescent pregnancy and women’s health provisions are important, but in terms of establishing that some pro-life groups are operating in a way that is indistinguishable from Al-Qaeda, I do need to cover the basics.
When you look closely at the studies of adolescent pregnancies in the developing world you will see there are huge differences in rates of pregnancy depending on where you are looking. This is really important. We need to consider something called the Total Fertility Rate (the TFR), which is complicated calculation, ending with a number which suggests how many babies are born per 1000 women of that age bracket over a 36-month period right? So, as an example, the adolescent fertility rate in East Asia is 7.1, whereas the corresponding rate in Central Africa is 129.5. But this doesn’t tell the full picture. Five years ago, for example, the overall adolescent fertility rate in South-East Asia was a 33, but those rates varied from 0.3 in South Korea to 83 in Bangladesh. So you can’t simply designate a rate to a large region. And even within individual countries there are enormous variations; in Ethiopia, the percentage of girls under the age of 19 who have begun childbearing ranges from 3% in Addis Ababa to 23% in the Affar region.
Or in other words, saying ‘Well thats just what they DO in Africa’ or other such simplified nonsense, is actually very wrong. The rate of teenage pregnancies even in African nations vary, depending on the circumstances in differing parts of that nation. The evidence tells us that very young women getting pregnant isn’t down to culture (although that does play a role), and isn’t down to religion (although that also plays a role); but down to factors that are NOT unique to remote African/Asian communities. The factors that cause this stuff are found everywhere, in EVERY nation. Adolescent pregnancies are a global problem occurring in ALL nations, including yours. In whatever country you wish to talk about then, pregnancy in adolescents is being driven by some uniting factors that transcend culture/religion; for example adolescent pregnancies are far more likely to occur in marginalised communities, where they are commonly driven by poverty, lack of education and lack of employment opportunities.
Once you acknowledge that, you begin to see similarities in the causes of this issue. In many societies (and many groups within our societies), girls are under pressure to marry and bear children early from the adults of both sexes in their communities. In EVERY case where these girls choose (aka they seemingly give consent) to become pregnant so young, their choice is ALWAYS down to limited educational and employment prospects. Adolescents who decide they DON’T want to get pregnant in such communities, however, may not be able to do so due to knowledge gaps and misconceptions on where to obtain contraceptive methods and how to use them (someone decided they should not know about contraception).
Oh, and in case you were thinking I missed it, one of the principle causes of unintended pregnancy is sexual violence, with more than a third of girls in some countries reporting that their first sexual encounter was coerced. (Legal note: That’s taking on board that differing nations have differing definitions of the words coerced, after all, in some parts of the world you can still legally marry 12 year old girls. Oh, and by ‘some parts of the world’? I’m looking at you America. Not talking about some developing part of the world; I am looking at the United States and their sanctioned child marriage laws in many parts of their nation). Alright, so… Adolescent mothers (those aged 10–19 years) face significantly higher risks of eclampsia, puerperal endometritis and systemic infections compared to women aged 20–24 years. On top of this? There are an estimated 4 MILLION unsafe abortions performed globally on girls aged 15–19 years each year, contributing to maternal mortality, morbidity and lasting health problems.
And we know this situation gets compounded as the girls grow up. A recent report by UNFRA pointed out that women who began childbearing in adolescence, had almost five births by the time they reached 40. Among girls with a first birth at 14 or younger (!!!!), nearly three quarters also have a second birth later in adolescence, and 40 per cent of those with two births, progress to a third birth BEFORE leaving the teen years. At this point we can describe this as systemic abuse. Only we legally can’t, as most births among girls under the age of 18 in 54 developing countries we are talking about, are reported as being part of a marriage or a union, which corresponds with the global increase in child marriage rates. But this is not just a problem in the “developing world” anymore.
In fact the only reason I am citing the UN reports on the developing world is because the UN and WHO have been focused upon the developing world in the past as, you know, this kind of crap was seemingly not an issue in rich, developed nations. Until recently that is… (stares at the Supreme Court… just shakes his head). The executive direction of the UNFPA summed up the issue recently in very stark terms- “When nearly a third of all women in developing countries are becoming mothers during adolescence, it is clear the world is failing adolescent girls.” Dr. Natalia Kanem went on to say, “The repeat pregnancies we see among adolescent mothers are a glaring signpost that they desperately need sexual and reproductive health information and services.”  In case you haven’t picked up on a theme here, I am offering harsh evidence to prove that unintended pregnancy is a HEALTH issue, not a religious one, as well as building my case.
And this brings us to the current situation in the United States of America. When the case of Dobbs v Jackson Women’s Health was ruled upon by the United States Supreme Court recently, (some months after the ruling was leaked) you saw an explosion is visceral anger to be sure, but I was immediately drawn to the responses of the people who had spent the last few decades in the front line, dealing with the above? As I said, almost all reports from the World Health organisation and the UN agencies deal with ‘developing’ nations and not ‘developed’ nations as basically, looking after woman's health is one of the criteria to kinda qualify as a developed nation in the first place. So, what was the response of the agencies who are in the front line of these issues, to Dobbs V Jackson Women’s Health? Simply, they said that it is “inevitable that more women will die, as restrictions by national or regional governments increase.”
That’s it. More women die when you restrict abortion. Be it in America or anywhere. As UNFPA said, “Whether abortion is legal or not, it happens… Data show that restricting access to abortion does not prevent people from seeking abortion, it simply makes it more deadly” According to the agencies’ 2022 “State of World Population” report, nearly half of all pregnancies worldwide are unintended, and over 60 per cent of these may end in abortion. All UNFPA said was that it feared that more unsafe abortions will occur around the world if access becomes more restricted. “Decisions reversing progress gained have a wider impact on the rights and choices of women and adolescents everywhere”. The decision in the United States will have a global repercussion (see below). Now, given the tone of Imgur since the ruling, at this point I know I am kinda preaching to the converted here; the Pro-Choice inclination of the community is well documented. I am not here to argue the moral imperative of being pro-choice to folks who have already agreed upon it.
But everything I’ve mentioned above contains little nuggets; key words and ideas, that are really significant. I write about geopolitics and history. That’s my bag. It’s what I have been doing for the last few years. So, lets talk about this in terms of geopolitics and history then shall we? Because from a geopolitical point of view? Dobbs v Jackson isn’t an aberration at all. It’s part of a systematic, organised, and deadly campaign by a bunch of dedicated, fanatical religious extremists, that has been taking place all over the world, and has finally managed to land a killing blow in the United States. No, I am not exaggerating. Allow me to offer proof. Now we have established just how much access to reproductive services is such a matter of literal life and death globally, we need to look at how these American extremists are having a global effect, specifically triggering deaths around the world. These are groups who are killing adolescent girls, maybe not intentionally, but that hardly makes it any better now does it?
Now, what many who criticise the U.S.A do not realise is that the United States is THE largest contributor to global public health efforts on Earth. By a large margin. This commitment, this willingness to help improve the health of people all over the world, has stood as one of the great beacons of America. It’s one thing to claim you are the defenders of liberty, but you know what? Investing literally billions of dollars to help improve the health of men, women and children across the globe is a powerful and incontestable vindication of a nations character. By this feat alone, America shows they wear the white hat; they can see themselves as the good guys. Except when it comes to reproductive rights. For years there have been provisions and clauses added to US foreign aid policy by elected officials who adhere to the teachings of extremist Christian groups, to limit access to women’s health based upon the tenants of their extremist belief. These elected US officials have been adding provisions and clauses into US aid that limits and bans funding to anyone who is involved in the delivery of abortions.
This reached a peak during the administration of Donald Trump, when in 2017 he imposed even more stringent rules on US health aid. It cut any funding from the United States to any charitable organisation that offered, or appeared to promote, abortion or even made reference to abortion, even if just marginally. Again, and this must be stressed, we are describing the largest donor of money to helping the world medically, focusing all its diplomatic attention specifically upon issues related to women’s health. As such, the 2017 ruling by President Trump had a profound geopolitical impact across the world; for example, it directly led to agencies offering contraceptive services to Malawi, Senegal and Kenya having to close down. Indeed, if we look closer at what these ‘rules’ did to Africa, you can begin to understand the power and influence of these extremist Christian groups, and I can begin to show how they CAN be accused of simply murdering young women. Specifically, we need to look at what happened in Northern Nigeria because of Trump pandering to these extremist Christians.
While accessing health care in Nigeria isn’t always easy (given the government only spends 4% of its federal budget on health services), women in Northern Nigeria have additional problems when it comes to accessing health services. They need charity groups. And the charity groups got funding from the US because abortion is illegal in Nigeria except in the case of a direct risk to the mothers life, so the traditional triggers that stopped funding insisted upon by extremist Christians were never hit. These charitable clinics then had to deal with an additional problem- 44% of Nigerian girls are married BEFORE their 18th birthday, with the majority of that number being found in the regions of Northern Nigeria they were working in; given the health risks involved (see above), and the ban on abortion, the groups realised the best way to prevent a women’s health crisis would be to make sure the girls/women of the region had access to effective and safe contraception. But given they can only turn up irregularly (as this is a wild region, with groups like Boko Haram running around), they needed a simple but workable solution. The result was they began offering three-month implants for women to use as birth control. This became a popular service.
Trump’s 2017 decision to change US aid policy meant these charities, because they also operated in nations who did have more liberal laws on abortion, immediately lost their funding. Which prevented this service from continuing. Which resulted in a local pregnancy spike, in a region where the women clearly did NOT want to get pregnant. The overall result? In 2019 a study by The Lancet proved that this policy of Trump’s spectacularly BACKFIRED, as it contribution to a 40% INCREASE in the number of abortions due to the cutting back on contraception provision. This would be funny… except… as abortion is illegal there, there is a growing shadow economy in abortion provision, where badly trained providers kill around, on average, 6,000 Nigerian women each year. A 40% spike in abortions in northern Nigeria would have seen a 40% rise in the number of women and girls killed. Or in other words- the beliefs of these extremist Christians in the United States, _directly_ caused the deaths of innocent women and girls in places like Northern Nigeria. I am sure they will say ‘Well, technically it was Trump…’ but no. American ‘Pro-Life’ groups? J‘accuse!
Now, while Biden has undone the damage caused by Trump’s decision, the recent ruling by SCOTUS sends a strong message out on behalf of those extremists. Returning to the nations of Africa as an example, placing women’s health on the political agenda there hasn’t been easy. It’s an ongoing struggle, and in the last decade alone we saw a dozen African nations finally lift and change pre-colonial abortion laws. The forces which resist such things in those nations (almost always patriarchal, reactionary and dangerous) have now been emboldened by the SCOTUS ruling. What is worse is that ruling can and WILL be used to influence all future US foreign aid. But these American extremist groups do not just use indoctrinated American politicians to take their crusade across international borders alone. Those organisations have spent at least $58 million dollars over the decade 2008 and 2018 secretly campaigning against all sex education, contraception provision and abortion rights right across Africa. And they have also spent nearly as much trying to influence Europeans the same way. On no, their campaigns against the health of women in America is not just limited to the United States alone- it is a global crusade on their part. Or, put even more brutally- you know those headline figures I started this with, highlighting the impact of unwanted pregnancies on adolescents in the ‘developing world’? If we ask,” Why DO some girls in Africa get pregnant so young?” We can now say, with full confidence, that yes, there ARE local tribal and religious and cultural reasons for this, but it’s also because rich American Christians spend millions to make sure the situation never changes.
We see this proven in Uganda, which has one of the highest rates of teenage pregnancy on the continent, where ‘health clinics’ linked to far-right fundamentalist American groups, were opposed to giving out even basic contraception and openly lied about health risks to scare girls from taking them, let alone contemplate abortions. These American groups are not alone however. A proposed law in Kenya that tried to expand abortion services and other health provisions across the nation was met with a ferocious online backlash by thousands of Kenya’s… which turned out to be a legion of bots and fake accounts run by an extremist Christian group in Spain. On the one hand, we can talk about the geopolitical implications to this (and we will) but a brief note- by studying the campaigns of lies, intimidation and manipulation carried out by American extremist religious groups in Africa, we can glimpse some of the tactics they are going to use in America. I think it now must be taken as granted that online comments in support of the SCOTUS ruling could be deeply infected with fake accounts and bots, like the campaign in Kenya, and that bot farming should be expected to play a role in all future campaigns to enact stricter legislation around abortion.
We have also just discovered, that the SCOTUS vote was the direct result of a long running multi-year secret campaign by members of these extremist groups to target, influence and groom the members of the SCOTUS. As recently revealed whistleblowing evidence released on Politico make clear (see links below), they got away with this due to the Justices setting their own ethical standards, which means the secret meetings, gifts and meals with the members of extremist sectarian Christians all took place under the radar. Basically these groups are powerful and entrenched. We will see this via the continued power of the elected officials who have been indoctrinated by these people and who will use their influence upon the foreign policy of the United States where they can. In many ways, the litmus test in this regard, is CEDAW
CEDAW is the global Convention on the Elimination of All Forms of Discrimination Against Women? It was an international treaty covering the treatment of women, drafted in 1979. That was 43 years ago. In that time it has been signed and ratified by virtually all nations on Earth. You know who have never signed up to CEDAW? The Vatican, the Revolutionary Islamic Republic of Iran, Somalia, Sudan and Tonga. You know who signed up for it but have NEVER ratified it? Palau in the Pacific, and… the United States of America. The failure to ratify CEDAW remains an indicator of the influence of these extremist Christian sects within US politics, and of how long their influence has been active. For such groups America must NEVER ratify CEDAW as article 16 of the treaty protects women’s rights to decide freely and responsibly on the number and spacing of their children and article 12 covers the right to health, which includes bodily autonomy and encompasses women’s and girls’ sexual and reproductive freedoms.
Returning then to geopolitical considerations, the SCOTUS ruling will make it harder for US legislators to resist the impact and influence of the religious extremists in this midsts. This will embolden these groups and allow them spread their influence internationally. For a direct comparison of what this looks like, we must turn to Saudi Arabia. In 1979, the Saudi government cut a deal with the extremists Wahhabi clerics of their nation to maintain the social status quo in their country. Part of this deal was to grant them control over Saudi’s international aid budget. Saudi, as one of the richest nations on Earth, was a key contributor to charity and aid to a plethora of Arab nations across the world. Only in the hands of extremist fundamentalists, this aid came with provisions. To get the aid, they had to promote the Saudi hard-core version of Islam. This spread of Wahhabi belief, or as it became known, Petro-Islam, became one of the contributory factors in the rise of Salafist extremism across the MENA (Middle East/North Africa) region, up to and including, the formations of Al-Qaeda in Sudan and the Taliban in the refugee camps of Pakistan. By the same measure, Christian extremists within the United States seek to fund, directly, similar-to-them extremist groups in nations across the world, and by having access to US federal aid donations, these American extremists gain a powerful back door across borders, manipulating other nations domestic policies via aid and offering powerful support for foreign allies. Extremist religious organisations across Africa and Asia can expect a possible influx of millions of dollars in US Aid.
Let us be clear- while such extremist Christians will claim passionately that their agenda is primarily driven by wider religious beliefs, it is focused, with laser like precision on reducing the availability and quality of health services available to women around the world, and, as the links below show, is based upon a campaign of outright lies and the refusal to accept responsibility for the consequences of their actions. It is a fairly safe speculation to assume that their aim would be to use the United States vast diplomatic and economic power to spread their extremist teachings across the world supplementing their multi-million dollar personally funded campaigns; they, like the adherents of Wahhabi Islam, don’t just want recruits, they also want influence.
Be that as it may, we need to return to my central proposition: So, in my best Will McAvoy channelling Aaron Sorkin voice, lets examine what we have before us. *A series of organisations whose guiding ideology is based on extremist, Sectarian, Christian beliefs, marking them technically, an extremist religious movement embedded within the political landscape of the United States of America. *Have a history of having violent extremists identify with their cause, but always giving themselves enough room to claim the violence had nothing directly to do with them. *Have been courted by, and indoctrinated, political leaders. *Have used the influence of these political leaders to enact changes in their nations foreign policy. *These changes can be directly linked to the deaths of young women across the world; if it is a globally recognised fact that the number one killer of adolescent girls is a preventable health problem, and you seek to stop that prevention, then you become a factor in these deaths. *They have used rich donors to fund and maintain a secretive campaign designed to spread their extremist beliefs across international borders, which will also contribute to the preventable deaths of young women.
Then let’s call them for what they are- An American al-Qaeda. There are differences of course. They do not use suicide bombers. They do not need to. They use legislation to kill their victims. Because let me make this clear- the difference between a religious movement who uses explosives to kill people and those who use legislation to kill people, is simple; one group can afford to wear nice suits. But both feel justified by their personal readings of their religious beliefs in what they do. The American version is, of course, much better organised than the Muslim Al-Qaeda ever was. Osama would have loved to have legislators who could influence Saudi’s foreign policy; the chances are had Osama been able to change the laws of Saudi as he originally wanted, he would have never needed to use direct violence.
Like Al-Qaeda today, these extremist Christian groups have extensive operations across Africa, and Asia, but unlike any Muslim group who displayed this level of international operation, causing this many deaths, said extremist Christian groups will not face House or Senate investigation, nor will they be placed upon the list of international terrorist organisations. They are incredibly successful in this respect. In fact from the perspectives of a global geopolitical position, it would be fair to say that their success only promises to grow. They are immune to arrest, prosecution, oversight or investigation within the territory of the United States, and any attempts to arrest members anywhere else in the world would be spun as it being the ‘persecution of Christians’. In truth the only solution lies within the hands of the majority of American’s who don’t share these extremist beliefs. Alas, also, even here the chances of victory also seem small. These groups are well entrenched, they have been operating for years and already have a siege mentality; they have also began radicalising the citizenry for an inevitable retribution response from the American electorate.
It must be said now, that while NOT originating within the anti-abortion movement, Christian sects must now come to terms with the existence of a violent new movement who claims an American Christian identity (Christian Nationalism); this pseudo-religious movement is actively grooming future violent extremists, and if this was a Muslim organisation in a foreign country, we would clearly be saying they are ready to use violence to maintain what power they have, and to seize state power at a future. It’s just that no one wants to say they are that far gone. But they are that far gone. They ARE that radicalised. They ARE that dangerous. Like Al-Qaeda they groom the weak and the scared into becoming soldiers for them. Like them, they are preparing for a religious war, during which they will start by saying they are the victims and maintain that line, even as their allies murder hundreds of women by legislation.
Again, huge numbers of Christians who describe themselves as Pro-Life across America would never, could never and will never tolerate the violence or rhetoric of Christian Nationalist groups, or would be sickened at idea that some Pro-Life groups would deliberately lie to teenage girls across the world. Regardless however, those members of this faction are in the same camp as these extremists now, and their choices, their individual choices, are stark. Either they walk away from the movement, oppose it and fight it until such elements are removed, or maintain their membership knowing they are tainted by the same brush. It’s a tough choice, but it’s one millions of Muslims have had to make for decades now. Non extreme Pro-Life advocates are now facing the fact that with this ‘victory’ in the SCOTUS ruling, the full range of people ‘on your side’ is being exposed and are having to choose if they can continue to support this cause. And cannot complain if they are judged for choosing to side with these people. If you lie down with dogs etc…
If this was an analysis of Iraq, Libya, Afghanistan, Ethiopia, any other nation, then anyone matching the mentality, beliefs, resources and ideology of these anti-abortion extremists would be identified as the single most dangerous threat to the status quo of the nation and constitute an existential threat to the future of that nation. I can offer no advice to Americans, except that it is only through electoral power than you will be able to legally and non-violently erode the gains these people have made, and that via these means you can slowly, and systematically, degrade their power.
To those not in America, as well as preventing our nations falling victim to such groups, we need to identify, isolate and sanction these organisations, even if they are American. We must facilitate class-action lawsuits where women in developing nations whose lives have been altered and irrevocably changed by the direct actions of these organisations, can find legal recourse and significant financial restitution from these groups- from their leaders; from the churches who support them and from the members of those churches. We need to facilitate these trials, shaming these people. Like all pseudo-terrorists everywhere, they wish to spin their actions as being explained solely within the context of their belief; what they will not want (and thus what we must use against them) is the shame of having their faith based beliefs reduced to a technical description of what they are- fraud; lies; murder; the abuse of children. This is what they fund, this is what they support, this is what we need to go after them on. Otherwise, these beliefs of theirs will spread, funded by rich donors and backed by the might of US Diplomacy. By then it will be too late to stop them.
In fact I think it is already too late. The most controversial part of this post isn’t me declaring the Christian groups who have worked so hard to end Roe v Wade are an American Al-Qaeda; it is to say they are five jumps ahead of us, are preparing for a war, and seem to be holding all the cards right now. It’s the fact I am saying they are winning. Look at them… and give the Devil his due… He has His shit together right now doesn’t he?
Please note- I AM very much aware of the nuance and subtle (and sometimes not so subtle) differences within the wide depth of Christian faith in the United States. The difference between the Evangelical movement and the Fundamentalist movement for example; the differences WITHIN the Evangelical movement; how there are still large numbers of liberal Southern Baptists for example, and so on. I am acutely aware of these differences and also the vast range of theological positions taken by Christians over the issue of abortion. However, I am also aware of the subtle differences within the vast number of folks who are Muslim also. And I find myself, after over a decade of trying to combat sweeping, ignorant generalisations towards Muslims by American Christian’s, now in a position where I know American Christian’s will want me to show them the same courtesy. After decades of refusing to listen to ‘Not all Muslims’ some will insist upon, ‘Not all Christians’. The Academic in me, the empathic person who studies faith from a position of respect, even if I don’t share that faith, will of course always react against sweeping simplifications and generalisations. These often cause more harm than good. By the same measure- some of these beliefs ARE killing young women across the world right now. They are. There is no way to soft soap this. I know that as I describe some of the tactics used by Christian groups above there will be many Christians who will look at them and be sickened. But to this, I can only say that the choices for those Christian’s who do not support such actions, are the same Muslims faced some years ago when facing extremists in their communities.
The response from normal Muslims globally has been firm. Absolute global condemnation against their extremist groups, a relentless campaign of preaching against the values of these groups, a long standing commitment to provide information to security services about the members of these groups and (if required) a willingness to bear arms against members of these groups when they, inevitably, resort to violence. That has been the universal approach towards radical and extremist Muslims by the global Muslim community. We should expect no less from Christians. Sorry, it is time for moderate Christians to start bearing their teeth.
I have been VERY precise in my labelling and will explain the full context of my use of the terms I used above; *Extremist (being pro-Life is NOT automatically an extremist opinion; however if the passion of this belief makes you a) excuse (not carry out, simply excuse) the use of violence (for example on health workers); or b) make ANY attempt to justify pregnancy due to acts found abhorrent by cultural norms (rape, incest, child sexual abuse); or c) seek to justify the refusal of medical treatment even in a life threatening situation (preventing an abortion that can save a women’s life) then you are an extremist, as no sane person on Earth would ever seek to justify that without some belief system having taken their thinking far beyond acceptable cultural norms. As such, ‘extremist’ views here are identified as being shared by mainline and successful evangelical preachers, pastors, politicians and faithful across America, as well as within Catholic circles; infecting all aspects of the ‘Pro-Life’ movement; to quote back an anti-Muslim speaker at these folks- ‘Not all Pro-Life supporters are Christian extremists; but all Christian extremists are Pro-Life’). Hence why I use the term Christian extremist.
What follows are links too back up every single point I made above. Please pursue them at your own time. “The world is failing adolescent girls”- the UNFRA press release that backs up their new report, “Motherhood in Childhood” https://news.un.org/en/story/2022/07/1121972 And a link to that report- Motherhood in Childhood is a 70 page dissection of the issues regarding global adolescent pregnancy. https://www.unfpa.org/publications/motherhood-childhood-untold-story And for those who don’t want to read all 70 pages, here is the executive summery: https://www.unfpa.org/resources/motherhood-childhood-untold-story-executive-summary The World Health Organisations resource page for Adolescent Pregnancy: https://www.who.int/news-room/fact-sheets/detail/adolescent-pregnancy The importance of women’s rights, including reproductive rights, to modern economies- this was written by the IMF ten years ago and still stands as the blueprint of any subsequent arguments as to its importance. https://www.imf.org/external/pubs/ft/fandd/2012/03/revenga.htm UNFPA in action- looking at the case study of Angola, where tacking girls health and reducing pregnancy, coupled with increasing economic opportunity for them and THEN prosperity for all. https://esaro.unfpa.org/en/news/tackling-one-worlds-highest-teenage-pregnancy-rates-give-girls-better-future I keep mentioning UNFPA- The Population Fund; established in 1969, it’s main duties include being the agency that tracks population/census figures and covers reproductive health. FYI- The Kemp-Kepstein Act (see blow) means UNFRA goes through cycles; when a Democrat is in charge, America funds it; when a Republican is in charge, the funding stops. https://www.unfpa.org/about-us They are important and righteous dudes (of all genders). Religious extremists have spent years carefully grooming the Conservative members of the SCOTUS: https://www.politico.com/news/2022/07/08/religious-right-supreme-court-00044739 The United States is the largest single contributor to help global health issues by a large margin: https://donortracker.org/united-states/globalhealth Studying the impact of Donald Trump’s ‘Global Gag Rule’ on health care providers on the matter of abortion: https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/full/10.1080/26410397.2020.1794412
The impact of banning abortions in Africa; harrowing accounts of life where abortions are illegal: https://www.africanews.com/2021/10/02/stigma-chokes-access-to-safe-abortion-in-kenya/ “A report released last year by the non-profit Center for Reproductive Rights (CRR) estimates that seven women and girls die every day in Kenya due to unsafe abortions.” An introduction and guide to both the Global Gag Rule (the executive order), and the Kemp-Kepstein Amendment (Congressional Amendment to appropriations legislation), and why this means that the UNFRA stops getting US funding every so often: https://res.cloudinary.com/dhu2eru5b/images/v1630408139/websites/pai2020/KempKasten-GGR-_FINAL/KempKasten-GGR-_FINAL.pdf From 2017; a detailed look at the implication of the reactionary Christian belief inspired Global Gag Rule’s impact upon the communities of northern Nigeria: https://www.theguardian.com/global-development/2017/jul/09/trump-abortion-crackdown-risks-stoking-nigerias-population-boom The Lancet report that shows the policy of Trump caused a 40% increase in abortion? Right here: https://www.thelancet.com/journals/langlo/article/PIIS2214-109X(19)30267-0/fulltext Recorded cases of women in Nigeria being refused access to contraception services as well. https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/23419997/ Anti-Abortion Groups in the United States (in this case Heartbeat International of Ohio) revealed to be funding a highly organised campaign of disinformation and lies in 18 nations across the developing world: https://www.opendemocracy.net/en/5050/trump-linked-religious-extremists-global-disinformation-pregnant-women/ An introduction to how extremist Christian groups in America use their money and their influence in US aid policy to devastate women’s health services in Africa: https://foreignpolicy.com/2022/06/01/roe-wade-us-abortion-ban-africa-global-gag-rule/ US funded woman's health centres in Uganda condemned for lies and misinformation: https://www.opendemocracy.net/en/5050/top-ugandan-health-official-condemns-us-linked-crisis-centres-for-opposing-contraception-for-pregnant-teens/ Spanish group funds a sock puppet campaign against reform of women’s health services in Uganda: https://mg.co.za/africa/2022-03-02-how-a-spanish-right-wing-group-incited-a-moral-storm-in-kenya/ US extremist groups spending over US$50m on trying to subvert/buy and influence political events in Europe: https://www.opendemocracy.net/en/5050/revealed-trump-linked-us-christian-fundamentalists-pour-millions-of-dark-money-into-europe-boosting-the-far-right/ The New York based Femm app and their campaign to team up with the Vatican to promote the app to advocate against the use of birth control in a nation where women rarely have control of their sex lives: https://www.theguardian.com/society/2019/jul/15/femm-menstruation-tracking-app-nigeria-anti-pill-campaignersus- From 2006; looking at the influence of evangelical Christians influence on US foreign policy at the time, including this quote; “During Bush’s time in office, U.S. aid to Africa has risen 67 percent, including $15 billion committed over five years for programs to fight HIV and AIDS. The policy has faced criticism from some health advocates who say its emphasis on abstinence at the expense of efforts such as condom distribution has slowed the response to fighting the disease”. https://www.cfr.org/backgrounder/christian-evangelicals-and-us-foreign-policy A deep dive into the growing threats posed by Christian Nationalism: https://www.americanprogress.org/article/christian-nationalism-is-single-biggest-threat-to-americas-religious-freedom/ An academic analysis of Christian Nationalism as it pertains to a) being identified as a violent religious movement and b) how it’s similarities with similar Muslims groups go beyond the superficial: https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s11109-021-09758-y And that’s it. Times are hard, and I do not seek anyone in this climate to spend money they do not have; look after yourself and your loved one first and foremost. If you are able, and you fancy buying me a coffee, you can make a contribution here. But no worries if you can’t. Just be excellent to one another my dudes. https://buymeacoffee.com/Thefeck
item.dc:creator: thefeckamisigningupwithfacebook
